combinations.narrative: You brace the mason jar against your knee and work the knife tip through the cork stopper, twisting slowly until the blade bites a narrow hole through the center. Crude, but wide enough for a wick.

You brace the mason jar against your knee and work one of the bent nails through the cork stopper, twisting slowly until the steel bites a narrow hole through the center. The shaft is crooked, but it does the work. Crude, but wide enough for a wick.

You uncork the mason jar and wet a fold of cloth with lamp oil. Working in small circles, you lift the tarnish from the brass until the Texas Ranger star catches the light again - sharp, deliberate, and recently cared for, as though someone polished it before hiding it here and the mountain only half forgot.

The badge looks the same in every line and curve. Only the surface has changed: dull bloom traded for a bright, honest mirror of whoever holds it.
